failed_rules.message: LoanType must be Closed End Second for 2nd Lien program
Property type 'Residential 5-8 Units' not eligible for DSCR - Investment. Available: SFR, PUD, Warrantable Condo, 2-4 Units
New Jersey is not eligible for 2nd liens in wholesale/retail channels
US Citizens are not eligible for Select ITIN program.
Occupancy 'Investment Property' not eligible. Available: Primary Residence
Super Jumbo program only available for Primary (Owner Occupied) occupancy - Non-Owner Occupied not eligible
Multi-Family 5-8 Units is not eligible for Investor DSCR - refer to DSCR Multi (5-8 Units) program

Property type 'Residential 5-8 Units' not eligible for Foreign National, Investment Property, FICO Score 784. Available: Single Family, PUD, 2-4 Units, Warrantable Condo, Non-Warrantable Condo, Condotel
US Citizens are not eligible for Foreign National program - FAIL
Property type 'Residential 5-8 Units' not eligible for Investment Property. Available: SFR, PUD, Warrantable Condo, Non-Warrantable Condo, Co-op, Condotel, 2-4 Units
Property type 'Residential 5-8 Units' not eligible for Investment Property. Available: SFR, PUD, Warrantable Condo, 2-4 Units
Property type 'Residential 5-8 Units' not eligible for Investment Property. Available: SFR, PUD, Warrantable Condo, 2-4 Units
Multi-Family 5-8 Units is not eligible for Investor DSCR No Ratio - refer to DSCR Multi (5-8 Units) program

specifications: Must be current, unexpired government-issued photo identification
Must show borrower full legal name matching loan application
Must include photo, signature, and date of birth
Acceptable forms: Driver's license, passport, state ID, military ID
For entity borrowers: ID required for all signing members with 25%+ ownership

reasons_needed.reason: Borrower identity verification required for all loan transactions per regulatory compliance
Name on ID must match loan documents, title, and entity documentation for DSCR Multi program

specifications: Must be fully executed with borrower signature and date
Must include correct borrower SSN and current address
Must specify tax years to be transcripted (most recent 2 years minimum)
Must identify lender/servicer as authorized recipient
Form must be signed within 120 days of closing

reasons_needed.reason: Required for all NQMF loan programs per Full Documentation guidelines
Enables IRS transcript verification to validate any tax return documentation if requested

document_requests.document_name: Non-Owner Occupancy Certification / Investment Property Affidavit
specifications: Must certify subject property will NOT be occupied by borrower or family members
Must confirm property will be held for investment/rental purposes only
Must include borrower signature and date
Must acknowledge property cannot be occupied by any LLC member per DSCR guidelines
Must disclose borrower's current primary residence address

reasons_needed.reason: DSCR program requires property cannot be occupied by borrower(s), any member of borrower's LLC, or any family member
Non-owner-occupied status triggers DSCR Multi (5-8 Unit) program eligibility and LTV limits
Investment property classification affects reserve requirements and documentation rules

document_requests.guideline_reference: DSCR RATIO - Property cannot be occupied by borrower(s), any member of the borrower's LLC or any family member
document_requests.document_type: Non-Owner Occupancy Certification / Investment Property Affidavit

specifications: Must be a tri-merge or Residential Mortgage Credit Report (RMCR) from Experian, TransUnion, and Equifax
Must include valid Social Security number verification for all borrowers
Must show credit scores from all three bureaus to determine qualifying score
Must include complete tradeline details with payment history and account status
Must show all inquiries, public records, disputes, collections, and charge-offs
Must certify results of public record searches for each city of residence in last 2 years
Must be dated within 120 days of closing
Must verify all credit references provided on loan application
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require tri-merge credit report for all borrowers on the loan
Credit score of 784 must be verified to confirm program eligibility for DSCR Multi 5-8 Units
Tradeline requirements must be verified: 3 tradelines for 12+ months OR 2 tradelines for 24+ months with recent activity
Credit report needed to identify any credit events, housing events, or derogatory items
Required to verify no active bankruptcy, foreclosure, or short sale issues
Public records search required to verify borrower identity and residency history

specifications: Must document borrower's current primary residence address
Must provide proof of ownership (deed, mortgage statement) OR active lease agreement
Primary residence should be in different geographic location from subject property OR demonstrate superior appeal/location
If renting, lease must be current and active
Must support non-occupancy claim for subject investment property

reasons_needed.reason: DSCR Housing History Verification requires borrowers who own a Primary Residence must provide proof of ownership
Primary residence documentation supports investment property occupancy classification
Geographic separation or superior appeal of primary residence validates non-occupancy of subject

document_requests.guideline_reference: HOUSING HISTORY VERIFICATION (DSCR ONLY) - Primary Residence requirements

specifications: Must be titled 'Business Purpose & Occupancy Affidavit' or equivalent form
Must identify all borrowers by full legal name
Must identify the subject property address: 5-8 unit residential property in New Jersey
Must declare the property is for commercial business or investment purpose only
Must affirm the property will not be occupied by borrower(s), any member of borrower's LLC, or any family member
Must be signed and dated by all borrowers
Must be consistent with non-owner occupied status declared on 1003 application
Must reconcile with DSCR Multi (5-8 Units) program occupancy requirements
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require Business Purpose & Occupancy Affidavit on all Business Purpose loans
All DSCR loans must be Business Purpose and processed as non-TRID transactions per guidelines
Investment property/DSCR loan requires borrower declaration that property is for investment purpose only
Affidavit confirms property will not be occupied by borrower or family members as required for DSCR eligibility
Required for non-TRID transaction compliance and regulatory documentation
document_requests.source_guideline: NQMF Business Purpose Forms: Business Purpose & Occupancy Affidavit is required on all Business Purpose loans. All borrowers are required to sign to declare that the property is, or will be, for commercial business or investment purpose only.
document_requests.trigger: DSCR Multi (5-8 Units) program - all DSCR loans are business purpose loans requiring this affidavit

specifications: Must be titled 'Compliance Agreement – DSCR Loans' or equivalent acceptable form
Must identify all borrowers on the loan
Must identify the subject property address
Must document borrower(s) agreement to furnish the Lender and its successors/assigns with current copies of all Lease Agreements within a reasonable time upon request
Must be signed and dated by all borrowers
For correspondent transactions, must be provided at time of purchase; for retail/wholesale, executed at closing
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require Compliance Agreement – DSCR Loans or equivalent form on all DSCR transactions
Agreement ensures lender can request and receive current lease documentation post-closing
Required for ongoing servicing and investor compliance on DSCR loans
5-8 unit property requires lease documentation accessibility for all units
document_requests.source_guideline: NQMF Business Purpose Forms: Compliance Agreement – DSCR Loans or equivalent form, is required. Form is borrower(s) agreement to furnish the Lender and its successors and/or assigns with current copies of all Lease Agreements within a reasonable time upon request.
document_requests.trigger: DSCR Multi (5-8 Units) program requires DSCR Compliance Agreement per guideline

specifications: Must cover minimum 12 months of payment history on existing mortgage being refinanced
Must show payment dates and amounts for each monthly payment
Must clearly identify any late payments (30, 60, 90, 120+ days)
Must identify the property address and loan account number
Must be from current mortgage servicer or reflected on credit report
Must demonstrate 0x30x12 payment history if relying on mortgage history for tradeline requirement
Must show current loan balance and payment status
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require verification of borrower's housing history for all programs
Cash-out refinance requires verification of acceptable payment history on existing mortgage
Housing history with 0x30x12 can satisfy tradeline requirements per NQMF guidelines
Late payments on existing mortgage may affect eligibility or pricing for cash-out refinance
Required to verify no housing events (120+ day late payments) within seasoning periods

document_requests.document_type: Form 1025 Small Residential Income Property Appraisal Report
specifications: Must include Form 1025 as part of the appraisal for 5-8 unit property
Must reflect estimated market rent for all units
Must reflect terms of current lease if property is tenant-occupied
Must identify monthly gross rental income for DSCR calculation
Must account for any management fee reflected on appraisal when calculating qualifying rents
Market rents must be used for DSCR ratio calculation
reasons_needed: DSCR ratio calculation requires gross rental income divided by PITIA
Multi 5-8 unit rental income requirements specify use of lower of estimated market rent or lease agreement on refinance
Form 1025 is the applicable FNMA form for 5+ unit properties to document market rent
Management fees reflected on appraisal must be deducted from qualifying rents per NQMF guidelines
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Guidelines - MULTI 5-8 UNIT RENTAL INCOME REQUIREMENTS; DSCR RATIO section

specifications: Must provide executed lease agreements for all occupied units in the 5-8 unit property
Must show tenant name and unit number for each lease
Must show monthly rent amount for each unit
Must show lease term with no less than 1 month remaining at time of closing
For month-to-month tenancy, must provide signed extension letter or bank statements to support continuance
Must not be a single room occupancy or boarder lease arrangement
Lease terms must match what is reflected on Form 1025
reasons_needed: Refinance transactions on DSCR loans require property to be leased
Qualifying rents use lower of estimated market rent or lease agreement amount on refinance
Month-to-month tenancy requires evidence of continuance per NQMF guidelines
Single room occupancy and boarder leases are not permitted on DSCR loans
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Guidelines - LONG TERM RENTALS – DSCR SUPREME AND INVESTOR DSCR; DSCR RATIO section

specifications: Must provide evidence of rent loss insurance coverage for the subject property
Coverage must equal at least 6 months of PITIA
Policy must name the subject property address
Blanket policies covering the subject property are eligible
Must be in effect at closing
Must cover the subject property
Coverage amount must equal at least 6 months of PITIA
Blanket policies covering subject property are acceptable
Must show named insured, property address, coverage amount, and effective dates
Must be signed and dated by agent
reasons_needed: Rent loss insurance for subject property is required on DSCR loans per NQMF guidelines
Minimum coverage of 6 months PITIA is required to protect rental income stream
Ensures continuity of income if property becomes vacant
Rent Loss Insurance is required for all DSCR loans per NQMF guidelines
Minimum coverage of 6 months PITIA protects income stream
Ensures mortgage payment can continue if rental income is interrupted
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Guidelines - RENT LOSS INSURANCE (DSCR) section

specifications: Must cover most recent 2 months (60 days) of account activity
Must include all pages even if blank
Must show borrower name as account holder
Must show financial institution name and logo
Must show account number (full or last 4 digits)
Must show beginning and ending balances for each statement period
Must show complete transaction history
Must be dated within 90 days of note date per NQMF guidelines
Statements for all accounts being used for closing costs and reserves required
Online statements acceptable if they contain all required elements
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require assets dated within 90 days of note date
Cash-out refinance requires verification of funds for closing costs and prepaid items
Investment property on 5-8 unit multi-family requires reserve verification
Account ownership must be confirmed to match borrower on loan application
Transaction history required to identify any large deposits requiring sourcing
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF ASSETS: Loan files must evidence sufficient funds from acceptable sources for down payment, closing costs, prepaid items, debt payoff, and applicable reserves

specifications: Must show sufficient verified liquid assets to meet reserve requirement
Must cover minimum 6 months PITIA reserves for investment property 5-8 units
Bank statements showing current balances acceptable for reserve verification
Must identify account type and confirm funds are liquid/accessible
Must show borrower name matching loan application
Reserves calculation based on PITIA (Principal, Interest, Taxes, Insurance, Association dues)
Must be from acceptable asset sources per NQMF guidelines
reasons_needed: Investment property with 5-8 units requires reserve verification per NQMF guidelines
DSCR loans on multi-unit properties typically require 6+ months reserves
Non-owner-occupied properties have higher reserve requirements than primary residence
Reserves must come from verified liquid assets, not gift funds per NQMF guidelines
Cash-out refinance on investment property requires post-closing liquidity verification
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF ASSETS: Loan files must evidence sufficient funds from acceptable sources for applicable reserves; GIFT FUNDS: Gift funds may not be used to meet reserve requirements

document_requests.description: Multi 5-8 Unit Residential Appraisal Report (FHLMC Form 71A or 71B)
specifications: Must be completed on FHLMC Form 71A or 71B (Narrative report acceptable for loan amounts $750,000+)
Must identify subject property address matching loan file
Must identify property as 5-8 unit multi-family residential
Must show appraised value to support LTV/CLTV calculation at 70%
Must be dated within 365 days of Note date; recertification required if exceeds 120 days
Must include full interior inspection of each unit
Must include rent roll for all units
Must include Income and Expense Statement
Must include photos of subject including exterior/interior and street scene
Must include aerial photo of subject property
Must include sketch or floor plan of typical units
Must include location map
Must include appraiser's qualifications and license/certification
Must disclose property condition rating (C5/C6 ineligible; C4 requires marketability review)
Must confirm property is free of deferred maintenance per DSCR requirements
Must confirm no health or safety issues, structural deficiencies, or fair/poor ratings
Must confirm minimum 400 square feet per unit with full kitchen and bathroom in each
Must confirm property usage represents highest and best use
Must include comparable sales analysis with map showing location of comparables
Must include appraiser certification and statement of limiting conditions
reasons_needed: Collateral valuation is required for underwriting all loan transactions
LTV/CLTV eligibility at 70% depends on appraised value
Multi 5-8 unit properties require specific FHLMC appraisal forms per NQMF guidelines
DSCR loans require property to be free of deferred maintenance
Property condition must meet minimum standards for program eligibility
Rent roll and income/expense data support DSCR calculation methodology
Subject property identity must be consistent across all loan file documents

document_requests.description: Commercial Broker Price Opinion or Field Review for Multi 5-8 Unit Property
specifications: Must identify subject property address
Must be completed based on sales approach methodology
Must review appraised value reasonableness
Must be completed by independent licensed professional (different from original appraiser)
If BPO value is more than 10% below appraised value, BPO value will be used for LTV purposes
In Pennsylvania or North Carolina, a commercial evaluation product is required instead of BPO
Field Review is acceptable alternative when available for Residential 5-8 Units
reasons_needed: NQMF Appraisal Review Process requires secondary appraisal product for Multi 5-8 Unit properties
Commercial BPO is required based on sales approach unless two full appraisals are provided
Value validation protects against collateral risk
10% tolerance rule may impact qualifying LTV

specifications: Must be issued by FEMA-authorized flood determination provider
Must identify subject property address
Must indicate flood zone designation (Section B4)
Must indicate whether property is in Special Flood Hazard Area (SFHA - Zone A or V)
Must indicate community participation status in NFIP
Must be current and valid as of loan closing
reasons_needed: Required to determine if flood insurance is mandatory for the subject property
Properties in Flood Zone A or V require flood insurance per NQMF guidelines
Properties in SFHA must be in NFIP-participating community to be eligible
Appraisal report flood zone must be reconciled with flood certificate

document_requests.description: Hazard Insurance Policy / Evidence of Insurance for 5-8 Unit Property
specifications: Must provide coverage against fire, lightning, explosion, windstorm, hail, smoke, aircraft, vehicles, riot/civil commotion
Must provide replacement cost coverage (actual cash value not acceptable)
Named insured must include all persons holding title to subject property
Must include standard mortgagee clause (loss payable clause not acceptable)
Property address must match note/security instrument
Must be effective for at least 60 days after funding date
Must include policy number, coverage amounts, deductible, premium amount
Must include loss payee clause naming lender
Must be signed and dated by agent
Must include insurance company and agent contact information
reasons_needed: Hazard insurance is required on all properties to protect collateral
Coverage must be replacement cost basis per NQMF requirements
Mortgagee clause ensures lender rights are protected
All title holders must be named insured to ensure borrower rights

specifications: Must identify borrower(s) as owner/vested party matching loan application
Must identify subject property address: 5-8 unit residential property in New Jersey
Must include complete legal description of the property
Must disclose all existing liens, including the mortgage being refinanced
Must disclose all judgments, easements, encumbrances, and title exceptions
Must list all Schedule B requirements and exceptions
Must show title insurance company name and commitment effective date
Must be dated within 90 days of closing
Must confirm proposed lien position (first lien) after payoff of existing mortgage
Must reconcile vesting with borrower name(s) on loan application
reasons_needed: Title must confirm ownership and insurable interest for underwriting approval
Vesting must match borrower identity on loan file for DSCR Multi refinance
All existing liens must be identified to verify payoff and new first lien position
Title exceptions and encumbrances may create closing conditions or affect insurability
Legal description must match property for 5-8 unit multi-family collateral
Cash-out refinance requires clear title path after existing lien payoff

specifications: Must be the recorded deed showing current ownership of the subject property
Must identify all vested parties by full legal name
Must match borrower name(s) exactly or include chain of title explanation
Must include complete legal description matching title commitment
Must show recording information (book/page or instrument number, recording date)
Must reflect current vesting type (individual, joint tenants, tenants in common, etc.)
Must be the most recent deed in the chain of title
reasons_needed: Confirms borrower is the legal owner of the 5-8 unit property being refinanced
Vesting must reconcile with title commitment and loan application
Required to verify ownership for DSCR Multi cash-out refinance eligibility
Legal description must match appraisal and title commitment for collateral verification
Underwriter must confirm borrower has authority to encumber the property

specifications: Must identify current mortgage servicer/creditor name and contact information
Must include loan/account number for the existing mortgage being paid off
Must show total payoff amount including principal, interest, and any fees
Must include good-through date (valid for at least 30 days from closing)
Must show per diem interest amount for payoff calculation
Must identify the subject property address securing the existing lien
Must include any prepayment penalty amount if applicable
Must include wire instructions or payoff remittance address
Must be dated within 30 days of anticipated closing
reasons_needed: Cash-out refinance requires payoff of existing mortgage to establish new first lien
Payoff amount is required to calculate funds to close and cash-out proceeds
Underwriter must verify existing lien can be satisfied at closing
Good-through date ensures payoff figures are valid through closing timeline
Per diem is needed for accurate final payoff calculation at settlement
Required to reconcile with title commitment showing existing mortgage lien

specifications: Must provide 2 months bank statements demonstrating receipt of rental income
Required only for month-to-month lease situations to support rental income continuance
Must show deposits matching or reasonably close to lease amounts
Must identify account holder name as borrower or borrower entity
reasons_needed: For leases converted to month-to-month, 2 months bank statements required to support rental income per Multi 5-8 Unit requirements
Bank statement deposits validate that rental income is actually being received
Supports continuance of month-to-month tenancy for DSCR qualification
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Guidelines - MULTI 5-8 UNIT RENTAL INCOME REQUIREMENTS
document_requests.conditional: Required only if any lease is month-to-month

document_requests.description: Submission Summary Report / UCDP Findings for Appraisal
specifications: Must reference the subject property appraisal
Must include FNMA SSR Score (2.5 or less acceptable per Appraisal Review Process)
Must identify any flags or warnings requiring resolution
Must be current with appraisal submission
reasons_needed: SSR/UCDP is standard appraisal review requirement
SSR Score of 2.5 or less is acceptable secondary appraisal product per NQMF Appraisal Review Process
Identifies data quality issues that may require appraisal corrections

document_requests.description: Assignment of Rents Rider (FNMA Form 3170) for Investment Property
specifications: Must be completed on FNMA Form 3170
Must identify subject property address
Must assign rents to lender as additional security
Must be executed by all borrowers on title
reasons_needed: 1-4 Family Rider (Assignment of Rents) is required for all Investment Properties per NQMF appraisal requirements
Provides lender with security interest in rental income stream
Required as additional collateral documentation for non-owner occupied properties

specifications: Must be the most recent monthly statement (within 60 days)
Must identify borrower name(s) matching loan application
Must identify current servicer and loan/account number
Must show subject property address
Must show current unpaid principal balance
Must show monthly payment amount (P&I, escrow breakdown if applicable)
Must show payment due date and any past due amounts
Must show interest rate on existing loan
reasons_needed: Verifies existing mortgage obligation on the property being refinanced
Supports reconciliation with payoff statement amount
Confirms housing payment history for DSCR Multi qualification
Unpaid balance used to verify cash-out refinance loan amount calculation
Required to verify existing lien matches title commitment disclosure
